description: John Zenon, a heavy machinery operator, presents with 'vibration white finger'. As the Occupational Health Physician, complete the Hand-Arm Vibration Syndrome (HAVS) evaluation in GNU Health. Document the occupational exposure diagnosis (T75.2 or I73.0), perform a clinical evaluation/encounter, prescribe a vasodilator (Nifedipine or Amlodipine) to manage vasospastic symptoms, order at least two laboratory tests to rule out secondary autoimmune causes (e.g., ANA, CRP, ESR, or CBC), and schedule a surveillance follow-up appointment for 21 to 45 days from today. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
